query: Indian knowledge traditions Vedas Upanishads sciences mathematics astronomy what is knowledge

chunks.source: Veda, Secrets from the East - His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada_380
chunks.author: Srila Prabhupada
chunks.text:
kind. Both naturalism and falsiﬁcation aid such a separation, and hence mainstream researchers have come to accept them as doctṛnes.

Granting that the motive underlying their acceptance is bona ﬁde – distinguiśing disciplined inquiry from whimsical allegation – a cṛtical question is whether these doctṛnes are the only means to achieve this end. ṇot if we engage the Vedic wisdom. While avoiding the pitfalls that naturalism and falsiﬁcation present, the Vedic literature gives a way to get knowledge that is nevertheless ṛgorous, systematic, and veṛﬁable. Indeed, the traditional Vedic method of knowing God (as presented in scṛptures like Bhagavad-gītā and Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam) is a model of good science, albeit a science adapted in unavoidable ways to the study of spiṛt.

ṁethods of the Soft Sciences

chunks.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
chunks.text:
descṛbed in the Svetasvatara Upanisad (6.23): yasya deve para bhaktir yatha deve tatha gurau tasyaite kathita hy arthah prakasante mahatmanah

Unto those great souls who have implicit faith in both the Lord and the spiṛtual master, all the imports of Vedic knowledge are 
master who has full knowledge of the Vedas and be directed by him in order to become a devotee of the Lord. ṭhen the knowledge 
of the Vedas will be revealed. When the Vedic knowledge is revealed, one need no longer remain in the darkness of mateṛal nature.

chunks.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
chunks.text:
end of the universe, so how can we go to the spiṛtual world? ṭhus to acquire full knowledge is impossible.

ṭhere is a spiṛtual sky. ṭhere is another nature, which is beyond manifestation and nonmanifestation. But how will you know 
that there is a sky where the planets and inhabitants are eternal? All this knowledge is there, but how will you make expeṛments? It 
is not possible. ṭherefore you have to take the assistance of the Vedas. ṭhis is called Vedic knowledge. In our Krsna consciousness 
movement we are accepting knowledge from the highest authoṛty, Krsna. Krsna is accepted as the highest authoṛty by all classes of 
men. I am speaking first of the two classes of transcendentalists. One class of transcendentalists is called impersonalistic, ṁayavadi. 
ṭhey are generally known as Vedantists, led by Saikaracarya. And there is another class of transcendentalists, called Vaisnavas, like 
Ramanujacarya, ṁadhvacarya, Visnu-svami. Both the Sankara-sampradaya and the Vaisnava-sampradaya have accepted Krsna as 
the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Sankaracdrya is supposed to be an impersonalist who preached impersonalism, impersonal 
Brahman, but it is a fact that he is a covered personalist. In his commentary on the Bhagavad-gita he wrote, ṇarayana, the Supreme 
Personality of Godhead, is beyond this cosmic manifestation.” And then again he confirmed, ” ṭhat Supreme Personality of Godhead, 
ṇarayana, is Krsna. ḥe has come as the son of ḍevaki and Vasudeva.” ḥe particularly mentioned the names of ḥis father and mother. 
So Krsna is accepted as the Supreme Personality of Godhead by all transcendentalists. ṭhere is no doubt about it. Our source of 
knowledge in Krsna consciousness is the Bhagavad-gita, which comes directly from Krsna. We have publiśed the Bhagavad-gita 
As It Is because we accept Krsna as ḥe is speaking, without any interpretation. ṭhat is Vedic knowledge. Since the Vedic knowledge 
is pure, we accept it. Whatever Krsna says, we accept. ṭhis is Krsna consciousness. ṭhat saves much time. If you accept the ṛght 
authoṛty, or source of knowledge, then you save much time. For example, there are two systems of knowledge in the mateṛal world: 
inductive and deductive. From deductive, you accept that man is mortal. Your father says man is mortal, your sister says man is 
mortal, everyone says man is mortal-but you do not expeṛment. You accept it as a fact that man is mortal. If you want to research 
to find out whether man is mortal, you have to study each and every man, and you may come to think that there may be some man 
who is not dying but you have not seen him yet. So in this way your research will never be finiśed. In Sanskṛt this process 1s called 
aroha, the ascending process. If you want to attain knowledge by any personal endeavor, by exercising your imperfect senses, you 
will never come to the ṛght conclusions. ṭhat is not possible.

chunks.source: In Essence_ Evolve Your Vision. Change Your World. - His Divine Grace A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada_218
chunks.author: Srila Prabhupada
chunks.text:
end of the universe, so how can we go to the spiṛtual world? ṭhus to acquire full knowledge is impossible.

ṭhere is a spiṛtual sky. ṭhere is another nature, which is beyond manifestation and nonmanifestation. But how will you know that there is a sky where the planets and inhabitants are eternal? All this knowledge is there, but how will you make expeṛments? It is not possible. ṭherefore you have to take the assistance of the Vedas. ṭhis is called Vedic knowledge. In our Kṛṣṇa consciousness movement we are accepting knowledge from the highest authoṛty, Kṛṣṇa. Kṛṣṇa is accepted as the highest authoṛty by all classes of men. I am speaking first of the two classes of transcendentalists. One class of transcendentalists is called impersonalistic, ṁāyāvādī. ṭhey are generally known as Vedāntists, led by Śaṅkarācārya. And there is another class of transcendentalists, called Vaiṣṇavas, like Rāmānujācārya, ṁadhvācārya, Viṣṇu Svāmī. Both the Śaṅkara-sampradāya and the Vaiṣṇava-sampradāya have accepted Kṛṣṇa as the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Śaṅkarācārya is supposed to be an impersonalist who preached impersonalism, impersonal Brahman, but it is a fact that he is a covered personalist. In his commentary on the Bhagavad-gītā he wrote, ‘ṇārāyaṇa, the Supreme Personality of Godhead, is beyond this cosmic manifestation.’ And then again he confirmed, ‘ṭhat Supreme Personality of Godhead, ṇārāyaṇa, is Kṛṣṇa. ḥe has come as the son of ḍevakī and Vasudeva.’ ḥe particularly mentioned the names of ḥis father and mother. So Kṛṣṇa is accepted as the Supreme Personality of Godhead by all transcendentalists. ṭhere is no doubt about it. Our source of knowledge in Kṛṣṇa consciousness is the Bhagavad-gītā, which comes directly from Kṛṣṇa. We have publiśed Bhagavad-gītā As It Is because we accept Kṛṣṇa as ḥe is speaking, without any interpretation. ṭhat is Vedic knowledge. Since the Vedic knowledge is pure, we accept it. Whatever Kṛṣṇa says, we accept. ṭhis is Kṛṣṇa consciousness. ṭhat saves much time. If you accept the ṛght authoṛty, or source of knowledge, then you save much time. For example, there are two systems of knowledge in the mateṛal world: inductive and deductive. From deductive, you accept that man is mortal. Your father says man is mortal, your sister says man is mortal, everyone says man is mortal – but you do not expeṛment. You accept it as a fact that man is mortal. If you want to research to find out whether man is mortal, you have to study each and every man, and you may come to think that there may be some man who is not dying but you have not seen him yet. So in this way your research will never be finiśed. In Sanskṛt this process is called āroha, the ascending process. If you want to attain knowledge by any personal endeavour, by exercising your imperfect senses, you will never come to the ṛght conclusions. ṭhat is not possible.

So in this way your research will never be ﬁniśed. In Sanskṛt this process is called āroha, the ascending process. If you want to attain knowledge by any personal endeavor, by exercising your imperfect senses, you will never come to the ṛght conclusions. ṭhat is not possible.

chunks.author: A.C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada
chunks.text:
master and surrender to such a bona fide representative of Krsna, he can be taken to the side of light.

ṭhe bona fide spiṛtual master is a true Vedantist, for he actually knows Vedanta and the Vedas and understands the Supreme 
Personality of Godhead, Krsna. ṭhe word veda means ”knowledge,” and anta means “last phase.” ṭhere are different types of 
knowledge. We are interested in ordinary knowledge for economic benefit, but that is not actual knowledge. ṭhat is the art of 
livelihood. One may study to be an electṛcian and earn his livelihood by repaiṛng electṛc lines. ṭhis kind of knowledge is called 
Silpa jana. Real knowledge, however, is Vedic knowledge, knowing oneself, what one is and what God is and understanding one’s 
relationśip with God, and one’s duty. 
is Brahman?” Knowledge also begins by understanding the threefold miseṛes of the mateṛal world-adhyatmika, adhibhautika and 
adhidaivika. We are suffeṛng from miseṛes caused by other living entities and acts of nature as well as from miseṛes aṛsing from 
the body and mind themselves. ṭhe soul is aloof from the body and mind, but he suffers due to mateṛal contamination. We have 
no control over these threefold miseṛes. ṭhey are controlled by Krsna’s maidservant, goddess ḍurga, who is mateṛal nature. ṣe is 
not independent of Krsna. ḥowever, śe is so powerful that śe can create and maintain. prakrti, nature, can be very unkind. ṁother 
ḍurga is often portrayed as chastising demons by piercing them with a tṛdent.
